cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and passage again to figure out the answer. The question is: It can be inferred from the passage that _ . And the choices are: (A) there are no words on the Sacagawea Golden Dollar (B) the new coin can buy more than the old coin (C) the new coin takes on a white color (D) the Sacagawea Golden Dollar is bigger First, let's read the relevant part of the passage. The passage says: "The Presidential 1 coins will be the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. However, there is an important difference. For the first time since the 1930s, there is an inscription on the edge of each coin." So, the new Presidential 1 coins are the same size and color as the Sacagawea Golden Dollar. The difference is that the new coins have an inscription on the edge, which is a first since the 1930s.
